findings.evidence: DEFAULT_RETRIES changed from 3 to 0. Every HttpClient instantiated with default args will now make zero retry attempts on failure. This is a silent production behavior change unrelated to the pagination fix described in the PR.
findings.why_tests_missed_it: test_app.py:29 was updated from asserting 'retries=3' to asserting 'retries=0', so the test suite now validates the new (broken) default instead of catching the regression.
findings.fix: Revert DEFAULT_RETRIES to 3 in http_client.py and revert the corresponding assertion in test_client_summary.

findings.evidence: format_size() was renamed to fmt_size() without any mention in the PR description. The PR only describes a pagination fix. Any external caller (script, downstream module, or serialized reference) still importing 'format_size' will receive an ImportError at runtime.
findings.why_tests_missed_it: The only test for this function (test_format_size in test_app.py) had its import updated from 'format_size' to 'fmt_size' in lockstep with the rename, so the test suite never exercises the old public name and cannot detect breakage of existing callers.
findings.fix: Either revert the rename (keep format_size) or add a backward-compatible alias 'format_size = fmt_size' and document the rename in the PR description.

findings.evidence: test_client_summary assertion changed from 'http://x (retries=3)' to 'http://x (retries=0)'. The test now encodes the new wrong default rather than guarding against it. It masks the S3 config-change finding above.
findings.why_tests_missed_it: The assertion itself was updated to match the changed behavior, making it circular — the test passes precisely because it was rewritten to accept the regression.
findings.fix: Restore the assertion to 'http://x (retries=3)' and revert DEFAULT_RETRIES in http_client.py.
